title: cornus alba subsp. stolonifera
intro: Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera, commonly known as Red-stemmed Dogwood or Red-barked Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub belonging to the Cornaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The therapeutic actions of Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera include its ability to modulate the nervous system, lower blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents present in this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera has many benefits, such as its ability to provide antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body, potentially lowering the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, while its antimicrobial properties can help prevent infections and promote wound healing. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to have cardioprotective and neuroprotective effects, which can contribute to improved cardiovascular health and cognitive function. By incorporating Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and enhanced mental clarity, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
actions: Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reducing joint pain and swelling, allowing individuals to engage in daily activities with greater ease and comfort. Its antioxidant properties can also help protect against oxidative stress, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, giving individuals a more youthful and radiant appearance. Additionally, Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection, allowing individuals to recover from injuries more quickly and efficiently. By incorporating Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera into their daily routine, individuals can enjoy improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, leading to a better quality of life.
constituents: Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (kaempferol and quercetin), phenolic acids (salicylic acid and ferulic acid), alkaloids (cornine and berberine), and phenolic glycosides (cornusoside and loganin). These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress-related diseases. For instance, the flavonoids in Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera can help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while the alkaloids can exhibit antibacterial properties to combat infections. By harnessing these medicinal constituents, people can benefit from improved wound healing, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced overall well-being.
parts: Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera has many medicinal parts, such as its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids like chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid. The roots of Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera contain iridoids like cornuside andloganin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the plant's berries contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants responsible for their red color, and have been traditionally used to treat various health issues.
preparations: Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for various medicinal purposes. The tea made from the bark of Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera is used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and indigestion, due to its anthraquinone content. A tincture of the plant's roots is used as a diuretic to help reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections. Additionally, infusions of the plant's leaves and stems are used to treat fever and inflammation, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties.
side_effects: Cornus alba subsp. stolonifera can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive problems in some individuals. In severe cases, excessive consumption of the plant's berries can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, the plant's roots and stems may cause kidney irritation and inflammation if ingested in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or extracts can also trigger skin rashes and photodermatitis in sensitive individuals.
